description: The central feature of the face through which breath flows and scent is perceived. The nose bridges outer air and inner body, filtering and warming what we take in. It detects the invisible signatures of things—smoke, flowers, rain, danger—connecting us to the world through its most ephemeral dimension.
sound_symbolism: The word begins with 'n', the nasal consonant that physically resonates through the nose itself—saying 'no' requires the very organ named. The vowel 'o' rounds like nostrils opening. Then 'ha' /ɸa/ breathes out with the bilabial fricative, pure breath passing. Finally 'e' perceives, the moment scent becomes awareness.
grammatical_notes: Refers to the nose as physical feature and sensing organ. For breathing as action, use verb forms. For scent/smell as noun, see related vocabulary.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Breath awareness begins at the nostrils, the anchor point for meditation. The nose is gateway to mindfulness practice.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: The nose reads weather, danger, food safety, and health—knowledge carried in scent before written records. Traditional healing attends to breath quality.

tags.physical: Central facial feature and breath channel
